spec_match.rationale: The playbook now follows ChromeStatus: Chrome 138 is the final update stream for macOS 11, Chrome 139+ requires macOS 12+ for new installs, and existing macOS 11 users need upgrade guidance.

interactive.rationale: Audience, impact, milestone, detection method, visitor state, placement, cadence, tone, and banner date controls all feed live playbook, copy, and detection-snippet output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The generated output now matches the feature page's promise of a tailored communication and technical playbook for unsupported macOS 11 visitors.

edge_cases.rationale: The UI now exposes separate branches for existing macOS 11 installs, blocked new installs, supported macOS 12+ visitors, and unknown or unavailable client-hint detection.

references.rationale: The page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/4504090090143744 self-heal link.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps local styling on shared CSS variables, has paired button colors, and avoids static-card-only output.

openQuestions.title: Run the required chrome-devtools-mcp browser verification
openQuestions.detail: Chrome DevTools MCP is not exposed in this Codex session, so this source-review refresh cannot honestly claim the required browser pass. Before fully closing this critique, open /v139/chrome-removes-support-for-macos-11/replacement-playbook/ with chrome-devtools-mcp, exercise each select, the banner date input, generated copy, detection snippets, and /v139/chrome-removes-support-for-macos-11/conformance/, then record console, network, DOM/readout, and conformance-route evidence.

summary: Source review repaired the broken inline script, added visible visitor-state branches for the macOS 11 removal edge cases, and aligned the generated copy with ChromeStatus feature 4504090090143744. Official browser verification remains open because chrome-devtools-mcp is unavailable in this environment.
